---
title: "David's EDA"
format: html
---


## Intro

Your exploratory data analysis of the team datasets go here.
This is my QMD for my EDA for the project.

```{r, package}
library(here)
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)


# tech_2018_raw <- readr::read_csv(
#   here::here("data", "tech_2018_raw.csv"))
# 
# cs_panel_raw <- readr::read_csv(
#   here::here("data", "cs_panel_raw.csv"))

source(here::here("data", "data_cleaning.R"))
```

## Descriptive Statistics

```{r}
merged_panel %>%
  summarize(
    missing_ai_rate = sum(is.na(ai_adoption_rate)),
    nan_ai_rate = sum(is.nan(ai_adoption_rate)),
    zero_ai_total = sum(ai_firms_total == 0, na.rm = TRUE))

merged_panel <- merged_panel %>%
  mutate(
    ai_adoption_rate = ifelse(ai_firms_total == 0, NA, ai_adoption_rate))
```

## Graphs

```{r}
merged_panel %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = revenue)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 50) +
  scale_x_log10() +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Revenue (log scale)")

merged_panel %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = n_firms)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 50) +
  scale_x_log10() +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Revenue (log scale)")

merged_panel %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = n_emp)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 50) +
  scale_x_log10() +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Revenue (log scale)")

merged_panel %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = payroll_per_emp)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 50) +
  scale_x_log10() +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Revenue (log scale)")

## AI adoption at the core level:

merged_panel %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = ai_adoption_rate)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 50) +
  labs(title = "Distribution of AI Adoption Rate")

## time trend
merged_panel %>%
  group_by(year) %>%
  summarize(mean_ai = mean(ai_adoption_rate, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(year, mean_ai)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  labs(title = "Average AI Adoption Over Time")

## industry heterogeinity
merged_panel %>%
  group_by(NAICS2017) %>%
  summarize(mean_ai = mean(ai_adoption_rate, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  arrange(desc(mean_ai)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 10)


## state variation
merged_panel %>%
  group_by(state) %>%
  summarize(mean_ai = mean(ai_adoption_rate, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(state, mean_ai), y = mean_ai)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip()

## relationship with firm outcome
merged_panel %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = ai_adoption_rate, y = payroll_per_emp)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm") +
  scale_y_log10()


## AI vs. firm size
merged_panel %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = ai_adoption_rate, y = n_firms)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3) +
  scale_y_log10()

## revenue
merged_panel %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = ai_adoption_rate, y = revenue)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3) +
  scale_y_log10()


merged_panel %>%
  group_by(NAICS2017) %>%
  summarize(mean_ai = mean(ai_adoption_rate, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(NAICS2017, mean_ai), y = mean_ai)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "AI Adoption by Industry")


merged_panel <- merged_panel %>%
  mutate(
    share_high_ai = ai_firms_high_use / ai_firms_total,
    share_low_ai  = ai_firms_low_use / ai_firms_total
  )
ggplot(merged_panel, aes(x = share_high_ai)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 40)

merged_panel %>%
  count(state, NAICS2017) %>%
  summarize(
    min_years = min(n),
    max_years = max(n))


merged_panel %>%
  select(ai_adoption_rate, n_emp, revenue, payroll_per_emp) %>%
  cor(use = "complete.obs")

ai_motivations |> 
  group_by(motivation) |> 
  count()

ai_motivations |> 
    ggplot(aes(x = n_firms, y = n_emp, colour = motivation)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  theme_minimal()

```
